Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

  "file_path": "/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-cj1phacp/generate_service.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys
from pathlib import Path


RESTART_CHOICES = [
    "no",
    "on-success",
    "on-failure",
    "on-abnormal",
    "on-watchdog",
    "on-abort",
    "always",
]


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--name",
        required=True,
        help="Service name (without .service extension)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--exec",
        dest="exec_start",
        required=True,
        help="Command to execute",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--user",
        default="root",
        help="User to run the service as (default: root)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        help="Service description (default: '{name} service')",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Units to start after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        choices=RESTART_CHOICES,
        default="on-failure",
        help=f"Restart policy (default: on-failure, choices: {', '.join(RESTART_CHOICES)})",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        type=int,
        default=5,
        help="Seconds to wait before restarting (default: 5)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        help="Output file path (default: {name}.service)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="Target to install into (default: multi-user.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (repeatable)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        help="Working directory for the service",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(args):
    """Generate the systemd service unit file content."""
    # Set defaults that depend on name
    description = args.description or f"{args.name} service"
    after_units = args.after if args.after is not None else ["network.target"]
    output_file = args.output or f"{args.name}.service"

    lines = []

    # [Unit] section
    lines.append("[Unit]")
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    if after_units:
        lines.append(f"After={' '.join(after_units)}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Service] section
    lines.append("[Service]")
    lines.append(f"Type=simple")
    lines.append(f"User={args.user}")
    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec_start}")
    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    for env in args.env:
        if "=" in env:
            lines.append(f"Environment={env}")
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Skipping invalid env format (expected KEY=VALUE): {env}", file=sys.stderr)
    lines.append("")

    # [Install] section
    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")
    lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines), output_file


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    content, output_file = generate_service_file(args)

    output_path = Path(output_file)
    output_path.write_text(content)

    print(f"Generated: {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
import sys
from pathlib import Path


RESTART_CHOICES = [
    "no",
    "on-success",
    "on-failure",
    "on-abnormal",
    "on-watchdog",
    "on-abort",
    "always",
]


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files"
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--name",
        required=True,
        help="Service name (without .service extension)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--exec",
        dest="exec_start",
        required=True,
        help="Command to execute",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--user",
        default="root",
        help="User to run the service as (default: root)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description",
        help="Service description (default: '{name} service')",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--after",
        action="append",
        default=None,
        help="Units to start after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart",
        choices=RESTART_CHOICES,
        default="on-failure",
        help=f"Restart policy (default: on-failure, choices: {', '.join(RESTART_CHOICES)})",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--restart-sec",
        type=int,
        default=5,
        help="Seconds to wait before restarting (default: 5)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        help="Output file path (default: {name}.service)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--wanted-by",
        default="multi-user.target",
        help="Target to install into (default: multi-user.target)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env",
        action="append",
        default=[],
        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (repeatable)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--working-dir",
        help="Working directory for the service",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(args):
    """Generate the systemd service unit file content."""
    # Set defaults that depend on name
    description = args.description or f"{args.name} service"
    after_units = args.after if args.after is not None else ["network.target"]
    output_file = args.output or f"{args.name}.service"

    lines = []

    # [Unit] section
    lines.append("[Unit]")
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    if after_units:
        lines.append(f"After={' '.join(after_units)}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Service] section
    lines.append("[Service]")
    lines.append(f"Type=simple")
    lines.append(f"User={args.user}")
    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec_start}")
    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")
    for env in args.env:
        if "=" in env:
            lines.append(f"Environment={env}")
        else:
            print(f"Warning: Skipping invalid env format (expected KEY=VALUE): {env}", file=sys.stderr)
    lines.append("")

    # [Install] section
    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")
    lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines), output_file


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    content, output_file = generate_service_file(args)

    output_path = Path(output_file)
    output_path.write_text(content)

    print(f"Generated: {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
